title: The Falcon And The Dohman

jokes.text: Tonight we reveal to you the fate of Nedna. I have devoted my summer to a more meaningful task salvaging Mr. Popper's Penguins online.
jokes.setup: Opening with dramatic promise of revealing important plot information
jokes.punchline: Admitting he wasted time on internet reviews instead
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about creator priorities, mild self-deprecation

jokes.text: like a bread crumb lodged inside my stomach crease
jokes.setup: Describing how the answer is hidden in the episode
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific visual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and visceral imagery for a simple concept

jokes.text: Sneaking in at noon / Just in time for my lunch break
jokes.setup: Song lyrics about sneaking into work
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's arriving at noon for lunch break
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - lazy but somehow logical in his mind

jokes.text: I'm still drunk from last night / Got driven home / I don't know by whom
jokes.setup: Casual admission of being drunk
jokes.punchline: Escalating to not knowing who brought him home
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's complete irresponsibility

jokes.text: Some may say / I don't deserve any pay / But hey / I came up with '60s Day / Last May
jokes.setup: Acknowledging he doesn't deserve pay
jokes.punchline: Defending himself with one trivial contribution from months ago
jokes.explanation: Well-structured rhyme with perfect Homer logic - one minor idea justifies months of laziness

jokes.text: While I'm here, I might as well pre-punch out
jokes.setup: Homer just arriving at work
jokes.punchline: Immediately thinking about leaving
jokes.explanation: Economical and perfectly captures Homer's work ethic

jokes.text: Larry, my man! Hey, you're not Larry. How come you're not Larry?
jokes.setup: Homer greeting someone enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Asking why they're not someone else as if it's their fault
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - treating someone's identity as a personal failing

jokes.text: The warmth of human contact with a manly whiff of violence
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he likes fist-bumping
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of a simple gesture
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly eloquent description of masculine bonding

jokes.text: Uh, by the way, that's my 'he's.'
jokes.setup: Homer using 'he's' in conversation
jokes.punchline: Claiming ownership of the contraction
jokes.explanation: Minor meta joke about speech patterns

jokes.text: Crazy house. Violent ward.
jokes.setup: Homer asking where Larry is
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery of disturbing news
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of shocking information

jokes.text: Good ol' Larry.
jokes.setup: Learning Larry is in a violent psychiatric ward
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriately fond reaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious nostalgia despite horrific context

jokes.text: I couldn't help but notice that your breath doesn't smell like alcohol
jokes.setup: Homer making an observation about Wayne
jokes.punchline: Treating sobriety as unusual and noteworthy
jokes.explanation: Homer's workplace culture where drinking is the norm

jokes.text: As God as my witness, this fist will be bumped!
jokes.setup: Homer's failed attempts to connect with Wayne
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration over something trivial
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to make grand proclamations about minor things

jokes.text: Marge, your ratio of peanut butter to cracker was spot on
jokes.setup: Reality cooking show format
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic praise for basic snack preparation
jokes.explanation: Satire of reality TV's tendency to dramatize mundane activities

jokes.text: For your prize, you have won a brand-new kitchen, which I will now become
jokes.setup: Typical reality show prize announcement
jokes.punchline: Host transforming into kitchen equipment
jokes.explanation: Surreal transformation gag typical of Simpsons absurdism

jokes.text: You know I hate surprises. You tricked us. I like routine. I like routine.
jokes.setup: Lisa adding apple to snacks
jokes.punchline: Bart's extreme reaction to minor change
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's unexpected need for routine

jokes.text: I guess it's not much when you look at real problems in the world, like Major League umpires not using instant replay
jokes.setup: Homer putting his problems in perspective
jokes.punchline: His idea of 'real problems' is sports officiating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer perspective - attempting wisdom but revealing shallow priorities

jokes.text: No, I was tipsy last night when a telephone survey called, so I told them to call back tonight for more accurate answers
jokes.setup: Charlie declining to go for drinks
jokes.punchline: Overly responsible approach to telephone surveys while drunk
jokes.explanation: Absurd commitment to accuracy in trivial matters

jokes.text: Look, I'm not weird or anything, I just like to feel like I'm friends with everybody
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain his behavior
jokes.punchline: The explanation makes him sound exactly as weird as suspected
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of self-awareness about how he comes across

jokes.text: Oh! and I don't want you calling me at home and saying 'Turn on channel six.'
jokes.setup: Wayne setting boundaries with Homer
jokes.punchline: Very specific example of annoying behavior
jokes.explanation: Specific enough to feel real, shows Wayne knows Homer's type

jokes.text: But what if the weather girl...
jokes.setup: Wayne forbidding him from calling about TV
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately wanting to make an exception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - can't follow simple instructions for even 5 seconds

jokes.text: We're completing each other's... Completing each other's... sen... ten... ces...
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he and Wayne are finishing each other's sentences
jokes.punchline: He's actually just repeating himself and struggling with the word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - what appears to be connection is just Homer's confusion

jokes.text: Just, uh, lining up all the presidents, nice and neat
jokes.setup: Moe getting robbed and stalling
jokes.punchline: Obsessive organization while being held at gunpoint
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Moe's neuroses even under pressure

jokes.text: Thanks for the upgrade.
jokes.setup: Moe organizing money during robbery
jokes.punchline: Robber appreciating the service
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan moment of criminal courtesy

jokes.text: Well, we all know what we're doing. The question is, is it an appropriate reaction to the situation?
jokes.setup: Wayne about to fight robbers
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation mid-action scene
jokes.explanation: Unexpected philosophical moment from action character

jokes.text: Drunks are so boring.
jokes.setup: Robber dealing with drunk Homer during robbery
jokes.punchline: Criminal complaining about victim quality
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - robber as the put-upon party

jokes.text: My fresco! That's coming out of your stealings!
jokes.setup: Damage during the fight
jokes.punchline: Moe's made-up word 'stealings' and misplaced priorities
jokes.explanation: Great Moe moment - linguistic creativity and misdirected anger

jokes.text: Oh, speaking of which, let's get these eggs back in a jar
jokes.setup: Wayne being modest about heroic actions
jokes.punchline: Casually transitioning to cleaning up pickled eggs
jokes.explanation: Nice mundane follow-up to action sequence

jokes.text: Wow, that's the farthest that one of my eggs ever made it down someone's throat
jokes.setup: Cleaning up after the fight
jokes.punchline: Moe's inappropriately detailed knowledge of his food's journey
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross Moe observation with disturbing implications

jokes.text: Which actually comes up about once a week
jokes.setup: Marge saying anyone who saves Homer gets a free meal
jokes.punchline: Casual admission this happens constantly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway line revealing Homer's constant near-death experiences

jokes.text: Special training. The kind they're not allowed to give anymore
jokes.setup: Wayne being asked about his fighting skills
jokes.punchline: Ominous suggestion of government/military abuse
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor hinting at Wayne's mysterious background

jokes.text: I started drinking orange juice with pulp. I asked the Reverend, and he said it was okay. Then he hung right up on me. That man is cranky at 3:00 in the morning.
jokes.setup: Ned sharing about big life changes
jokes.punchline: Trivial change requiring religious consultation at inappropriate hour
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders - overthinking minor decisions and oblivious to social norms

jokes.text: as seen in this Taiwanese animated dramatization
jokes.setup: News report about the tavern incident
jokes.punchline: Reference to real-world phenomenon of Taiwanese news animations
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to viral internet news format

jokes.text: Thanks to you, Wayne, we did not lose the visible half of the moon
jokes.setup: Burns praising Wayne's heroism
jokes.punchline: Bizarre overstatement of what was at stake
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns absurdist logic connecting tavern robbery to cosmic consequences

jokes.text: I'm afraid that protocol demands that I totally erase your memory
jokes.setup: Burns praising Wayne
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to sinister mind control
jokes.explanation: Good Burns moment - casual mention of horrific corporate policy

jokes.text: Die, you fascist bastard! Mother, is that you?
jokes.setup: Wayne attacking Burns
jokes.punchline: Confusing Burns with his mother
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Wayne's psychological issues and Burns' maternal resemblance

jokes.text: I expect insolence, but rank insolence? Off with his job!
jokes.setup: Burns reacting to Wayne's attack
jokes.punchline: Distinguishing levels of insolence and medieval punishment phrasing
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns vocabulary and absurd hierarchy of offense

jokes.text: I have reoccurring flashbacks to a nightmarish past. That's funny, because I have reoccurring flash-forwards to a nightmarish future.
jokes.setup: Wayne sharing his psychological trauma
jokes.punchline: Homer relating with his own form of temporal anxiety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel structure showing both characters' different but relatable anxieties

jokes.text: He said it. Or, uh, maybe it was him. Man, this robot really gets me.
jokes.setup: Watching robot protesters
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion and emotional connection to robot
jokes.explanation: Homer's ability to relate to anything, even robots

jokes.text: Your voice is so gravelly, just like Lauren Bacall's
jokes.setup: Wayne having nightmares
jokes.punchline: Lisa's inappropriately sophisticated comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa moment - precocious cultural reference at wrong time

jokes.text: If you move that little top hat to St. James Place, we'll all be blown to kingdom come!
jokes.setup: Wayne's violent nightmare monologue
jokes.punchline: Monopoly reference mixed with death threats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist mixing of board game and spy thriller elements

jokes.text: Yeah, they couldn't find a Google map photo without me naked or urinating
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why Springfield doesn't appear on maps
jokes.punchline: His personal contribution to the town's obscurity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer explanation - personally responsible for town's problems

jokes.text: And when there was a mapmaker's convention here, they all got Lou Gehrig's disease. Not the one you're thinking of-- there's another one.
jokes.setup: Explaining Springfield's cartographic problems
jokes.punchline: Bizarre disease coincidence with confusing clarification
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation with confusing medical reference

jokes.text: I admit it! I let Milhouse lie down in your bed!
jokes.setup: Wayne demonstrating pressure point technique on Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart confessing to inappropriate but innocent behavior
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of interrogation technique with age-appropriate secret

jokes.text: Remember everything they say, and tell us later in rattle code
jokes.setup: Kids being sent away during adult conversation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate spy communication system
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - creating unnecessarily complex spy methods

jokes.text: If I told you, I'd have to kill you. Can I tell you?
jokes.setup: Wayne using classic spy movie line
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent enthusiasm for danger
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of cliché with Bart's typical response to threats

jokes.text: We can't keep serving the same thing every day. These kids have mashed potatoes coming out of their ears. Point taken.
jokes.setup: Complaint about repetitive school lunch menu
jokes.punchline: Literal mashed potatoes emerging from children's ears
jokes.explanation: Solid visual gag taking metaphor literally

jokes.text: This is the real world? Hallelujah! I'm still alive!
jokes.setup: Wayne's PTSD making him question reality
jokes.punchline: Finding Springfield to be an improvement over his expectations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wayne's relief at apparent normalcy

jokes.text: Ha-ha! And I'll appreciate every moment! Except the ones that aren't as good as they used to be. Which is all of them. Oh, someone kill me.
jokes.setup: Wayne's joy at being alive
jokes.punchline: Rapid descent into existential despair
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from joy to depression, good structure

jokes.text: ♪ Hiding in the basement / And I'm, like, where's the cat? / Where's, where's the cat? ♪
jokes.setup: Auto-tune disaster video
jokes.punchline: Absurdly mundane content given dramatic musical treatment
jokes.explanation: Satire of viral video culture and auto-tune overuse

jokes.text: You want to give it zero stars? No! We will go to... Springfield, America, and kill this man.
jokes.setup: Discussing rating the video
jokes.punchline: Massive overreaction to internet content
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mild criticism to international assassination plot

jokes.text: I see you are tight like borscht and beets
jokes.setup: Viktor describing Homer and Wayne's friendship
jokes.punchline: Culturally specific metaphor for closeness
jokes.explanation: Nice character-specific metaphor showing Viktor's background

jokes.text: No! Not the middle seat!
jokes.setup: Homer being kidnapped
jokes.punchline: Focusing on airline-style seating complaint during abduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priority confusion during crisis

jokes.text: What will you guys use when there aren't newspapers anymore? Perhaps we will be living in a world where there'll be no need to kidnap.
jokes.setup: Kidnappers using newspaper for proof of life
jokes.punchline: Discussion about media evolution and world peace during kidnapping
jokes.explanation: Great modern observation about changing technology and optimistic criminal

jokes.text: Oh, look-- here's a coupon for scissors that you have to cut out! Thanks, geniuses!
jokes.setup: Homer looking at newspaper during kidnapping
jokes.punchline: Pointing out logical flaw in coupon design
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about design flaws

jokes.text: This is no way to treat the talent
jokes.setup: Homer being roughed up by kidnappers
jokes.punchline: Treating kidnapping like show business
jokes.explanation: Homer's ego survives even kidnapping situations

jokes.text: I told you the bedspread smells funny
jokes.setup: Child at criminal hideout
jokes.punchline: Innocent complaint about accommodations
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family life in criminal environment

jokes.text: I left that out in a bowl and he ate them
jokes.setup: Marge asking how Homer got tracking chips
jokes.punchline: Homer's predictable eating of random electronics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - eating anything left accessible

jokes.text: Aah! I won a silver medal in Nagano-- ow!-- and now this!
jokes.setup: Random person getting caught in action sequence
jokes.punchline: Connecting past athletic glory to current misfortune
jokes.explanation: Good random character moment connecting achievements to current suffering

jokes.text: Stop it! Just let me put my hands under your armpits
jokes.setup: Homer being cold after action sequence
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate physical demands in serious moment
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate responses to physical discomfort

jokes.text: You ever eat on a train, Marge? What they call a steak is barely a hamburger.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Wayne living on a moving train
jokes.punchline: Homer's focus on food quality rather than lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - missing the point to focus on food complaints

jokes.text: Nobody held my space when I was in a North Korean prison being forced to write a musical about Kim Jung Il with a car battery hooked up to my nipples!
jokes.setup: DMV employee asking Wayne to hold his place in line
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and horrific backstory as justification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with incredibly specific absurd torture method, quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Nobody held my space when I was in a North Korean prison being forced to write a musical about Kim Jung Il with a car battery hooked up to my nipples!
jokes.setup: Wayne complaining about DMV service
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and dark comparison to North Korean torture
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd escalation from mundane complaint to torture musical

jokes.text: ♪ 'K' is Korea, just the north part / 'I' is for the Internet he bans ♪
jokes.setup: Musical number about Kim Jong Il
jokes.punchline: Cheerful alphabet song about oppression
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the musical torture concept with dark political humor

jokes.text: how come I wasn't an option in this contest?
jokes.setup: Contest about Ned and Edna's relationship
jokes.punchline: Skinner's delusional romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner moment showing his romantic delusions

jokes.text: Shh! Shush me again, and I'll take your head clean off.
jokes.setup: Someone shushing Wayne in theater
jokes.punchline: Violent overreaction to minor social correction
jokes.explanation: Good final character moment showing Wayne's inability to adjust to normal life

tmdb_overview: Homer befriends a new security guard at the plant; Marge daydreams about being a contestant on "Top Chef."
